page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g7.4xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs utilizing the x86_64 architecture, driven by an Intel Xeon 8369B processor running at 2.7 GHz. The system is equipped with 64.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 10 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations place the ecs.g7.4xlarge consistently in the average (middle 50%) performance tier across most compute and memory workloads. It achieved a single-core stress-ng score of 1889.28 and a multi-core score of 15481.55. Memory bandwidth tests show 835.37 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 94.57 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. In database operations, the instance processed 15.62 million Redis SET operations per second and scored 7892.12 in PassMark database benchmarks. Notably, it demonstrated strong performance in the top 25% tier for small-model LLM text generation, reaching 250.81 tokens per second with a 135M model, though medium-model (7B) text generation remains average at 11.22 tokens per second.

From a resource density perspective, the instance offers a balanced allocation of compute and memory resources, making it a qualitatively cost-efficient option for workloads that do not require specialized hardware accelerators or high-speed local NVMe storage. The primary tradeoff is the complete absence of local storage, which necessitates network-attached storage for persistent data. This configuration is appropriate for general-purpose workloads, including static web serving, software compilation, database operations, and moderate-scale language model inference.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g7.4xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance featuring 16 dedicated vCPUs and 16 physical cores on an Intel Xeon 8369B x86_64 processor running at 2.7 GHz. It is configured with 64.0 GB of RAM, providing 4.0 GB per core, and lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, while offering a 10 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark results place this instance in the average tier for most compute and memory tasks, including a PassMark CPU score of 21023.63 and a Redis SET throughput of 15.62 million operations per second. It exhibits strong performance in small-model LLM text generation at 250.81 tokens per second. Lacking local storage, it relies on network-attached storage, making it suitable for balanced workloads such as static web serving, software compilation, database operations, and general-purpose application hosting.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g7.4xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 16 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 10 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering balanced performance for database and web serving workloads.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g7.4xlarge features 16 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 10 Gbps bandwidth, suitable for general-purpose workloads.
tagline: A balanced x86_64 compute platform featuring dedicated Intel Xeon cores and 64 GB RAM for general-purpose enterprise workloads.

64.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core
10 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage or GPU
